behavior: Active, acrobatic seed-forager that clings to thistles and other seedheads; flies in a bouncy, undulating pattern and often calls in flight.
field_marks: Small finch with a short, conical bill and compact head.
Breeding male is bright yellow with a sharply black forehead.
Black wings show bold white wingbars and pale markings.
Short, notched black tail has conspicuous white patches in flight.
Females are duller yellow below and olive above; winter birds are unstreaked brown-gray with pale wingbars.
habitat: Weedy fields, open floodplains, forest edges, cultivated ground, orchards, and other semi-open places with thistles, asters, shrubs, or small trees.

sources.title: American Goldfinch Identification — All About Birds
